steps.result:
## Worldwide annual average monthly revenue per paying Netflix membership

| Year | USD per month | SEC validation / calculation |
|---|---:|---|
| 2019 | **$10.82** | Netflix 2020 Form 10-K: $19.859bn streaming revenue ÷ 152.984m average paying memberships ÷ 12 |
| 2020 | **$10.91** | Netflix 2020 Form 10-K: $24.757bn ÷ 189.083m ÷ 12 |
| 2021 | **$11.67** | Netflix 2023 Form 10-K: $29.515bn ÷ 210.784m ÷ 12 |
| 2022 | **$11.76** | Netflix 2023 Form 10-K: $31.470bn ÷ 222.924m ÷ 12 |
| 2023 | **$11.64** | Netflix 2023 Form 10-K: $33.640bn ÷ 240.889m ÷ 12 |
| 2024 | **$11.70** | Reported in Netflix’s 2024 Form 10-K performance highlights; also stated in the supplied article’s text, although omitted from its historical table |

Amounts are the rounded, worldwide **average monthly revenue per paying membership** for each full year.

### Reconciliation

For the years where the aggregate inputs are shown, Netflix’s metric reconciles as:

\[
\text{Average monthly revenue per paying membership}
=
\frac{\text{annual streaming revenue}}
{\text{average paying memberships}\times 12}
\]

For example:

- **2023:** $33,640.458m ÷ 240.889m ÷ 12 = **$11.637**, reported as **$11.64**.
- **2020:** $24,756.675m ÷ 189.083m ÷ 12 = **$10.910**, reported as **$10.91**.

The worldwide figure should **not** be calculated as a simple average of the four regional figures. It is effectively weighted by average paying memberships and revenue. Nor should the article’s December 31 year-end subscriber counts be used as the denominator: Netflix uses **average paying memberships over the year**, not year-end memberships.

## Definitions and caveats

Netflix defines a paid membership as:

> “a membership that has the right to receive Netflix service following sign-up and a method of payment being provided, and that is not part of a free trial or certain other promotions…”

The filings also state that a canceled membership stops being counted on its effective cancellation date. Memberships are assigned geographically according to the location used at sign-up, based on Netflix’s geolocation systems.

Beginning with the 2023 disclosure, Netflix additionally cautions:

> “Certain members have the option to add extra member sub accounts. These extra member sub accounts are not included in paid memberships.”

Accordingly:

- This is **revenue per paid membership/account**, not revenue per individual viewer or profile. A membership can serve more than one person.
- The figures are reported in **nominal U.S. dollars** and include actual foreign-exchange effects. They are not Netflix’s constant-currency growth rates.
- The numerator is streaming revenue, not merely the published subscription price. Netflix noted for 2023 that it also earned revenue from advertising, consumer products and other sources, although sources other than monthly membership fees were not material then.
- The metric reflects country mix, plan mix, price changes and exchange rates; it is not the price of a representative Netflix plan.

## Error in the supplied article

The article says that 2024’s **$11.70** was:

> “An increase of 35.89% since 2019.”

That comparison is incorrect:

\[
(11.70/10.82)-1 = \mathbf{8.13\%}
\]

The **35.89%** calculation instead matches the article’s **2016** value:

\[
(11.70/8.61)-1 \approx \mathbf{35.89\%}
\]

Thus the likely intended wording was “an increase of 35.89% since **2016**,” not since 2019. The article also omits the 2024 row from its worldwide historical table even though its surrounding text supplies the $11.70 figure.

### SEC sources

- Netflix, **2020 Form 10-K**, Item 7, “Results of Operations,” consolidated performance highlights — validates 2019 and 2020.
- Netflix, **2023 Form 10-K**, Item 7, “Results of Operations,” consolidated performance highlights — validates 2021–2023.
- Netflix, **2024 Form 10-K**, Item 7 performance highlights — validates 2024.  
- SEC issuer page: [Netflix filings, CIK 1065280](https://www.sec.gov/edgar/browse/?CIK=1065280&owner=exclude&action=getcompany).

steps.args: {'prompt': "From Netflix's 2024 10-K, extract the consolidated annual performance highlights for 2024, especially streaming revenue, average paying memberships, and average monthly revenue per paying membership. State exact numbers and the formula. Also identify comparative 2023 and 2022 metrics if shown. {{netflix_2024_10k}}"}
steps.result:
## Netflix 2024 consolidated annual performance highlights

From Netflix’s 2024 Form 10-K, Item 7, “Management’s Discussion and Analysis,” consolidated performance highlights table:

| Metric | 2024 | 2023 | 2022 | 2024 vs. 2023 |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| **Streaming revenues** | **$39,000,966 thousand** | $33,640,458 thousand | $31,469,852 thousand | **16%** |
| DVD revenues | $0 | $82,839 thousand | $145,698 thousand | (100%) |
| **Total revenues** | **$39,000,966 thousand** | $33,723,297 thousand | $31,615,550 thousand | **16%** |
| Operating income | $10,417,614 thousand | $6,954,003 thousand | $5,632,831 thousand | 50% |
| Operating margin | 27% | 21% | 18% | — |
| Paid net membership additions | 41,350 thousand | 29,529 thousand | 8,903 thousand | 40% |
| Paid memberships at year-end | 301,626 thousand | 260,276 thousand | 230,747 thousand | 16% |
| **Average paying memberships** | **277,730 thousand** | **240,889 thousand** | **222,924 thousand** | **15%** |
| **Average monthly revenue per paying membership (ARM)** | **$11.70** | **$11.64** | **$11.76** | **1%** |
| ARM constant-currency change | 4% | — | — | — |

Thus, in unscaled terms, 2024 streaming revenue was **$39.000966 billion**, average paying memberships were **277.730 million**, and reported ARM was **$11.70 per month**.

### ARM formula

For an annual period:

\[
\text{Average monthly revenue per paying membership}
=
\frac{\text{Annual streaming revenue}}
{\text{Average paying memberships}\times 12}
\]

Using the reported amounts:

- **2024**

\[
\frac{\$39{,}000{,}966{,}000}
{277{,}730{,}000\times12}
=\$11.702\approx\boxed{\$11.70}
\]

- **2023**

\[
\frac{\$33{,}640{,}458{,}000}
{240{,}889{,}000\times12}
=\$11.637\approx\boxed{\$11.64}
\]

- **2022**

\[
\frac{\$31{,}469{,}852{,}000}
{222{,}924{,}000\times12}
=\$11.762\approx\boxed{\$11.76}
\]

Netflix reports the revenue and membership figures in thousands, but the common scaling cancels in the calculation.

### Relevant qualifications

- Netflix discontinued its DVD-by-mail service during 2023, so **2024 streaming revenue and total revenue were identical**.
- Netflix said 2024 streaming revenue growth primarily reflected growth in average paying memberships and price increases, partly offset by unfavorable foreign-exchange movements.
- The reported **1% ARM increase** was **4% on a constant-currency basis**. Netflix calculates constant-currency ARM by translating current-period revenue using the corresponding prior-year monthly exchange rates and excluding revenue hedging gains or losses from both periods.
- Revenue from advertising, consumer products, live events, and sources other than monthly membership fees was **not material** in 2024, 2023, or 2022.

**Source:** Netflix, Inc. 2024 Form 10-K, Item 7, consolidated performance highlights and “Streaming Revenues,” pages 19–20, filed January 27, 2025.

{'final_result': '## Netflix average revenue per paying user, 2019–2024

Netflix calls this metric **Average Revenue per Membership (ARM)**: annual streaming revenue divided by average paying memberships and then divided by 12.

| Year | Average monthly revenue per paying membership | YoY change |
|---|---:|---:|
| 2019 | $10.82 | — |
| 2020 | $10.91 | +0.83% |
| 2021 | $11.67 | +6.97% |
| 2022 | $11.76 | +0.77% |
| 2023 | $11.64 | -1.02% |
| 2024 | $11.70 | +0.52% |

### Overall change

From 2019 to 2024, monthly ARM increased by:

- **Absolute increase:** $11.70 − $10.82 = **$0.88 per month**
- **Percentage increase:** ($11.70 ÷ $10.82 − 1) × 100 = **8.13%**
- **Five-year compound annual growth rate:** ($11.70 ÷ $10.82)^(1/5) − 1 = **1.58% per year**
- Annualized revenue per membership rose from approximately **$129.84** in 2019 to **$140.40** in 2024.

### Interpretation

Netflix’s revenue per paying membership increased overall, but the change was not linear. Most of the gain occurred in **2021**, when ARM rose 6.97%. It peaked at **$11.76 in 2022**, declined slightly in 2023, and recovered to **$11.70 in 2024**, still $0.06 below the 2022 peak.

The relatively modest reported-dollar growth reflects more than subscription-price changes: ARM is also affected by geographic and plan mix, advertising and extra-member revenue, and foreign-exchange movements. For example, Netflix reported that 2024 ARM increased only **1% on a rounded reported basis**, but **4% on a constant-currency basis**.

As a validation of the 2024 figure:

$39.000966 billion streaming revenue ÷ 277.730 million average paying memberships ÷ 12 = **$11.70 per month**.

**Bottom line:** Netflix’s worldwide average monthly revenue per paying membership rose **8.13%**, from **$10.82 in 2019 to $11.70 in 2024**, equivalent to a relatively modest **1.58% annualized growth rate**.
